2605.12966.title: Position: Agentic AI System Is a Foreseeable Pathway to AGI
2605.12966.paper_summary: Is monolithic scaling the only path to AGI? This paper challenges the dogma that purely scaling a single model is sufficient to achieve Artificial General Intelligence. Instead, we identify Agentic AI as a necessary paradigm for mastering the complex, heterogeneous distribution of real-world tasks. Through rigorous theoretical derivations, we contrast the optimization constraints of monolithic learners against the efficiency of Agentic systems, progressing from simple routing mechanisms to general Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) topologies. We demonstrate that Agentic AI achieves exponentially superior generalization and sample efficiency. Finally, we discuss the connection to Mixture-of-Experts, reinterpret the instability of current multi-agent frameworks, and call for greater research focus on Agentic AI.
2605.12966.paper_summary_zh: 單一擴展是達成AGI的唯一途徑嗎？本文挑戰了這一教條，即僅僅擴展單一模型就足以實現人工通用智能。相反，我們將代理AI確定為掌握現實世界任務複雜且異質分佈的必要範式。通過嚴謹的理論推導，我們對比了單一學習者的優化約束與代理系統的效率，從簡單的路由機制進展到一般的有向無環圖（DAG）拓撲。我們展示了代理AI在泛化能力和樣本效率上達到了指數級的優越性。最後，我們討論了與專家混合模型的聯繫，重新詮釋了當前多代理框架的不穩定性，並呼籲對代理AI進行更深入的研究。
2605.12966.author: Junwei Liao et.al.
2605.12966.authors: Junwei Liao, Shuai Li, Muning Wen, Jun Wang, Weinan Zhang
